---
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    css: ../course-outline.css
    slide-number: true
    center: false
    margin: 0.05
    footer: "<a href='javascript:history.back()'>← Back</a>"
---

```{=html}
<section class="slide level2">
<h2>VI. Jesus: The Only Way to the Father</h2>

<div class="lv-a"><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3V-OMl-Nxeo" target="_blank">Opening Song</a></div>
<div class="lv-a"><a href="https://bibleproject.com/videos/jesus-fulfills-the-law/" target="_blank">Overview Video</a></div>

<details>
<summary><strong>Opening Passage — John 14:6</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-lc"><a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+14%3A6&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 14:6</a> — "I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me."</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>A. Other Views</strong></summary>

  <details>
  <summary class="lv-1">Five Core Questions by Faith</summary>
  <div class="lv-lc">
  <p>1. Who is God?</p>
  <p>2. What is humanity's problem?</p>
  <p>3. How is salvation or liberation achieved?</p>
  <p>4. Who is Jesus?</p>
  <p>5. What is humanity's ultimate destiny?</p>
  </div>
  </details>

  <details>
  <summary class="lv-1">Comparison Chart — Slide 1</summary>
  <div class="lv-lc">
  <table><thead><tr><th>Faith</th><th>God</th><th>Humanity's Problem</th><th>Salvation / Liberation</th></tr></thead>
  <tbody>
  <tr><td>Judaism</td><td>One covenant God</td><td>Sin, covenant unfaithfulness</td><td>Repentance, Torah, mercy</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Christianity</td><td>Trinity</td><td>Sin and separation from God</td><td>Grace through Christ</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Islam</td><td>Allah</td><td>Disobedience</td><td>Submission and mercy</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Hinduism</td><td>Brahman</td><td>Ignorance and karma</td><td>Dharma, devotion, knowledge</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Buddhism</td><td>No creator God</td><td>Suffering and attachment</td><td>Eightfold Path</td></tr>
  </tbody></table>
  </div>
  </details>

  <details>
  <summary class="lv-1">Comparison Chart — Slide 2</summary>
  <div class="lv-lc">
  <table><thead><tr><th>Faith</th><th>Jesus</th><th>Ultimate Destiny</th><th>Rel.</th></tr></thead>
  <tbody>
  <tr><td>Judaism</td><td>not divine; not Messiah</td><td>World to Come</td><td>~</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Christianity</td><td>Son of God, Savior, Lord</td><td>Eternal life with God</td><td>✓</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Islam</td><td>Prophet and Messiah, not divine</td><td>Paradise or Hell</td><td>~</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Hinduism</td><td>Teacher/avatar (varies)</td><td>Moksha</td><td>~</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Buddhism</td><td>Enlightened teacher</td><td>Nirvana</td><td>✗</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Sikhism</td><td>Great teacher; not divine</td><td>Union with God; freedom from rebirth</td><td>~</td></tr>
  </tbody></table>
  <table><thead><tr><th>Symbol</th><th>Meaning</th></tr></thead>
  <tbody>
  <tr><td>✓</td><td>Personal relationship with God through Christ as adopted children of God</td></tr>
  <tr><td>~</td><td>Relationship with God/divine reality exists, but not the Christian concept of adoption through Christ</td></tr>
  <tr><td>✗</td><td>No personal Creator-God relationship as a central doctrine</td></tr>
  </tbody></table>
  </div>
  </details>

  <details>
  <summary class="lv-1">Eastern Religion Terms</summary>
  <div class="lv-lc">
  <table><thead><tr><th>Term</th><th>Religion</th><th>Meaning</th></tr></thead>
  <tbody>
  <tr><td><strong>Brahman</strong></td><td>Hinduism</td><td>Ultimate divine reality underlying all existence.</td></tr>
  <tr><td><strong>Avidya</strong></td><td>Hinduism, Buddhism</td><td>Ignorance of ultimate reality; the root error that keeps people in bondage.</td></tr>
  <tr><td><strong>Karma</strong></td><td>Hinduism, Buddhism, Sikhism</td><td>Actions and their consequences that shape one's future condition.</td></tr>
  <tr><td><strong>Dharma</strong></td><td>Hinduism, Buddhism</td><td>Duty, righteousness, or living according to the proper order of reality.</td></tr>
  <tr><td><strong>Bhakti</strong></td><td>Hinduism</td><td>Loving devotion to God.</td></tr>
  <tr><td><strong>Yoga</strong></td><td>Hinduism</td><td>Spiritual disciplines aimed at union with God or liberation.</td></tr>
  <tr><td><strong>Samsara</strong></td><td>Hinduism, Buddhism, Sikhism</td><td>The cycle of birth, death, and rebirth.</td></tr>
  <tr><td><strong>Moksha</strong></td><td>Hinduism</td><td>Liberation from samsara and union with ultimate reality.</td></tr>
  <tr><td><strong>Dukkha</strong></td><td>Buddhism</td><td>Suffering, dissatisfaction, unease, or the instability of earthly existence.</td></tr>
  <tr><td><strong>Tanha</strong></td><td>Buddhism</td><td>Craving, thirst, attachment, or clinging; the immediate cause of dukkha.</td></tr>
  <tr><td><strong>Nirvana</strong></td><td>Buddhism</td><td>The cessation of craving and suffering; freedom from rebirth.</td></tr>
  <tr><td><strong>Haumai</strong></td><td>Sikhism</td><td>Ego-centeredness; the illusion of self-rule that separates people from God.</td></tr>
  <tr><td><strong>Waheguru</strong></td><td>Sikhism</td><td>"Wonderful Lord"; a common Sikh name for God.</td></tr>
  </tbody></table>
  </div>
  </details>


</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>B. The Problem: Humanity's Fall into Sin</strong></summary>

  <details>
  <summary class="lv-1">The Fall</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+3%3A1&version=WEB" target="_blank">Genesis 3:1</a> — The serpent's deception; Adam and Eve disobey God.</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+3%3A8&version=WEB" target="_blank">Genesis 3:8</a> — They hid from God.</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">c. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+3%3A16-19&version=WEB" target="_blank">Genesis 3:16–19</a> — Sin brought separation and curse.</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">d. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+2%3A17&version=WEB" target="_blank">Genesis 2:17</a>; <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+5%3A12&version=WEB" target="_blank">Romans 5:12</a> — Spiritual and physical death entered humanity.</div>
  </details>
  <details>
  <summary class="lv-1">Sin separates us from God</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+59%3A2&version=WEB" target="_blank">Isaiah 59:2</a> — "Your iniquities have made a separation between you and your God, and your sins have hidden his face from you."</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+3%3A23&version=WEB" target="_blank">Romans 3:23</a> — "All have sinned and fall short of the glory of God."</div>
  </details>

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>C. God's Immediate Promise of Restoration</strong></summary>

  <details>
  <summary class="lv-1">The Protoevangelium — First Gospel (Genesis 3:14-15)</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis%20%203%3A14-15&version=RSV" target="_blank">Genesis 3:14-15</a> — "He shall bruise your head, and you shall bruise his heel." — The first promise of a coming Savior who would defeat Satan at great cost to Himself.</div>
  </details>

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>D. The Plan Foretold in Prophecy</strong></summary>

  <details>
  <summary class="lv-1">The suffering servant</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+53%3A4-6&version=WEB" target="_blank">Isaiah 53:4–6</a> — "He was pierced for our transgressions; he was crushed for our iniquities… the Lord has laid on him the iniquity of us all."</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+53%3A10-11&version=WEB" target="_blank">Isaiah 53:10–11</a> — "When his soul makes an offering for guilt… Out of the anguish of his soul he shall see and be satisfied."</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">c. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Psalm+22%3A1&version=WEB" target="_blank">Psalm 22:1</a>, <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Psalm+22%3A16-18&version=WEB" target="_blank">16–18</a> — "My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?" — quoted by Jesus on the cross (<a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+27%3A46&version=WEB" target="_blank">Matthew 27:46</a>).</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">d. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Zechariah+12%3A10&version=WEB" target="_blank">Zechariah 12:10</a> — "They shall look on me, on him whom they have pierced."</div>
  </details>

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>E. Why the Law Could Not Save Us</strong></summary>

  <details>
  <summary class="lv-1">The Law reveals sin but cannot justify</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+3%3A20&version=WEB" target="_blank">Romans 3:20</a> — "By works of the law no human being will be justified… through the law comes knowledge of sin."</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Galatians+3%3A11&version=WEB" target="_blank">Galatians 3:11</a> — "No one is justified before God by the law."</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">c. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Galatians+2%3A21&version=WEB" target="_blank">Galatians 2:21</a> — "If righteousness were through the law, then Christ died for no purpose."</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">d. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=James+2%3A10&version=WEB" target="_blank">James 2:10</a> — Breaking even one command makes us guilty of all.</div>
  </details>

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>F. Only Jesus Satisfies God's Justice</strong></summary>

  <details>
  <summary class="lv-1">Propitiation — Jesus bore our punishment</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+3%3A23-25&version=WEB" target="_blank">Romans 3:23–25</a> — "Justified by his grace as a gift, through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus, whom God put forward as a propitiation by his blood, to be received by faith."</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+Peter+3%3A18&version=WEB" target="_blank">1 Peter 3:18</a> — "Christ also suffered once for sins, the righteous for the unrighteous, that he might bring us to God."</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">c. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+6%3A23&version=WEB" target="_blank">Romans 6:23</a> — "The wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord."</div>
  </details>

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>G. Jesus' Exclusive Claim — Confirmed by the Apostles</strong></summary>

  <details>
  <summary class="lv-1">Jesus' own words</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+10%3A9&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 10:9</a> — "I am the door. If anyone enters by me, he will be saved."</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+3%3A36&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 3:36</a> — "Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life; whoever does not obey the Son shall not see life."</div>
  </details>
  <details>
  <summary class="lv-1">The apostles' testimony</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Acts+4%3A12&version=WEB" target="_blank">Acts 4:12</a> — "There is salvation in no one else, for there is no other name under heaven given among men by which we must be saved."</div>
  </details>

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>H. How We Come to the Father</strong></summary>

  <details>
  <summary class="lv-1">The way of salvation</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Acts+20%3A21&version=WEB" target="_blank">Acts 20:21</a> — Repentance toward God and faith in Jesus.</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+1%3A12&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 1:12</a> — Receiving Him as Lord and Savior.</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">c. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+3%3A3-7&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 3:3–7</a> — Being born again by the Holy Spirit.</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">d. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Ephesians+2%3A8-9&version=WEB" target="_blank">Ephesians 2:8–9</a> — "By grace you have been saved through faith… not a result of works, so that no one may boast."</div>
  </details>

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>I. Discussion Questions</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. Why is it significant that God promised a Savior immediately after the Fall in Genesis 3?</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. How does understanding the Law's purpose change how you see your need for Jesus?</div>
<div class="lv-1">3. What does "propitiation" mean, and why does it matter that God's justice was satisfied?</div>
<div class="lv-1">4. How do you respond to the claim that Jesus is the only way to God?</div>
</details>

</section>
```
